poems.title: Apartheid
poems.text:
America: Where
Apartheid never ended
we just renamed it.

poems.title: Crowns
poems.text:
they burned the books, and
lists of who'd ruled the world, we
wear Schrodinger's crown.

poems.title: Dreams
poems.text:
They took our
art, painted
horns so large
that no halos
could hang --
told the
children we were
demons, and then
blamed us for the
nightmares

poems.title: Footwork
poems.text:
Let me see
your foot
work --

Let's see how
far away we can
dance, tonight, maybe

dawn
won't be
able to
catch up...

poems.title: Freedom
poems.text:
Trying to paint you
within the margins
always felt like
an act of
oppression,
and you look so much
better free.

poems.title: Gravitas
poems.text:
they don't tell you how you'll
be damned to New Dante
because of how pain
looks so easy when you wear it; hung
like a poly-blend, draped
some-kind-of-casual across your
breastplate, all things
you are seen carrying
will be called weightless—

if you can dance through it,
the floor must be more
melody than molten —

if you can sing through it,
the air is clearly not choked with
smoke born of burnt soles danced
through the fires —

in the morning, when your mouth is dry,
and your lungs are full,
and your feet remind you of your Father's —
in the morning, when your moons have gone quiet —
in the morning, when your suns have died —
in the morning, they will judge you
for your proximity to
the dark matter as if you had
stolen their light by
remembering yourself.

poems.title: Out Loud
poems.text:
you were
not born
to be a
whisper, you
deserve to be
loved out loud.

poems.title: Stars
poems.text:
they cut us down from
the sky; we left the lights on
so we could find home

poems.title: Sun-Rises
poems.text:
i am tired of seeing,
of crying, of writing
our names out as eulogies,
as memories, as
mournings,

i'm tired of learning names
at sun set —
we are so often turned into
homegoings and
tear-torn celebrations
instead of
love letters, or hope
poems, or birthday songs

i am tired of saying
goodbye to people i should be
sending off with
a see-you-soon instead, i am tired
of saying goodbyes to people knowing
that in 28 hours another
of our bodies
will say hello to a God
they weren't expecting to meet,
and join a choir dotted with faces and names
they already know from the
last time their faces ran wet with
praise, and they danced through
eulogies,

i'm tired of knowing
that in twenty-eight hours
i will be back here
writing another poem
like this

poems.title: Treason
poems.text:
if you ask
my country the question and
just listen, she
will tell you

she will whisper it,
she will yell it, she will
show it in her
words and bullets and
mugshots and magazine
covers and public
policies and courtrooms gaveled
both by black cloth or public
opinion, she
will wax systemic for you
she will make it clear, just
listen to her screams, I know
you have long since stopped
listening to ours;

as a black American,
loving myself
is an act of
treason
